rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical oxidative sulfonylation/semipinacol ring-expansion of 1-(1-arylvinyl)cyclobutanols with arylsulfonyl hydrazides to give β-sulfonyl cyclopentanones.
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of iodide (NaI) to I2, which oxidizes ArSO2NHNH2 to a sulfonyl iodide that fragments to an arylsulfonyl radical; the resulting carbon radical from radical addition is further oxidized at the anode to a carbocation. Cathode reduces water to H2/hydroxide.

key_intermediates.name: tertiary carbon radical from ArSO2• addition to alkenyl cyclobutanol (II)

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes I- to I2, which converts ArSO2NHNH2 into ArSO2I and then an ArSO2• radical; the sulfonyl radical adds to the vinyl group of the cyclobutanol, the resulting C-radical is oxidized at the anode to a benzylic cation, which undergoes 1,2-alkyl (semipinacol) ring expansion of the cyclobutanol to give the cyclopentanone product. Cathode reduces water to H2 and hydroxide.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Only trace 3a formed with 3 equiv TEMPO, consistent with a radical intermediate.

probe_reagents.name: Ts-I (preformed sulfonyl iodide)
probe_reagents.observation: Ts-I with electrolysis gave 3a in 51%; even without current Ts-I afforded 3a in 35%, supporting ArSO2I as the on-cycle sulfonyl radical precursor.

notes: Desulfonation of 3l with Mg/MeOH gives α-methyl cyclopentanone 10, supporting the β-sulfonyl ketone connectivity. NaI acts as a redox mediator; without current, Ts-I still gives some product, indicating the electrochemistry mainly regenerates the I2/ArSO2I oxidant and oxidizes the C-radical to the cation for semipinacol rearrangement.
